message:
fix(harness): validate embedded scenario_id matches manifest map key (bd-242m8)

`validate_manifest` previously only checked scenario IDs at the map-key level. A
tampered or externally-produced manifest could embed a `ScenarioOutcome` whose
`scenario_id` field disagreed with — or was wholly invalid against — its
enclosing key, and the validator would still accept it. That weakened the audit
integrity guarantee for E2E artifacts: two manifests with identical map keys
could carry materially different scenario payloads.

This commit walks `(key, scenario)` pairs and:

- Adds `ManifestValidationError::ScenarioIdMismatch { key, value }` so the
  failure mode is distinct from `InvalidScenarioId` / `DuplicateScenarioId`.
- Validates the embedded `scenario.scenario_id` independently when it
  diverges from the map key, surfacing both `InvalidScenarioId` (if the
  embedded value is not a well-formed id) and `ScenarioIdMismatch`.
- Preserves the existing key-level validity and duplicate checks unchanged.

Tests:

- `embedded_invalid_scenario_id_detected` — embeds `"BAD"`, expects both
  `InvalidScenarioId("BAD")` and a `ScenarioIdMismatch`.
- `embedded_scenario_id_mismatch_detected` — embeds a syntactically valid
  but wrong id, expects only `ScenarioIdMismatch`.
- The exhaustive Display matrix is extended with a `ScenarioIdMismatch`
  variant so the `Display` round-trip stays complete.

The corresponding bead `bd-242m8` is added to `.beads/issues.jsonl` to keep
issue state synced with the change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+{"id":"bd-242m8","title":"Validate embedded artifact scenario IDs","description":"Artifact manifest validation checks scenario map keys but not ScenarioOutcome.scenario_id values. A tampered or externally-produced manifest can pass with a valid key and invalid or mismatched embedded scenario_id, weakening audit integrity for E2E artifacts. Validate both key and embedded value and reject mismatches.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T17:29:04.625457069Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T17:29:04.625457069Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","harness","validation"]}

 {"id":"bd-25k","title":"ext4: Implement group descriptor checksum verification hooks (metadata_csum)","description":"Goal: prepare for ext4 metadata checksum verification (CRC32C) when the filesystem uses metadata_csum.

Deliverables:
- Determine checksum algorithm and seed for group descriptors (from legacy ext4 code) including how group number participates.
- Add a function: verify_group_desc_checksum(desc_bytes, group_nr, superblock) -> Result<()>.
- If metadata_csum is not enabled, skip verification.

Acceptance:
- Unit tests: known-good descriptor passes; flipped-bit fails.
- Error type is stable and mapped correctly for user-facing surfaces.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:17:25.017305716Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T21:06:44.617157237Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T21:06:44.617138091Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","ondisk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-25k","depends_on_id":"bd-1a9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:18:06.845984197Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25k","depends_on_id":"bd-28h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:32:02.431265758Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-25k","depends_on_id":"bd-2cn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:18:06.766942705Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
